pests.name: Whitefly
pests.impact: Transmits Leaf Curl Virus (LCV), causing upward curling of leaves, shortening of internodes, distortion, stunted plants, and deformed fruits.

pests.name: Aphids
pests.impact: Transmits Papaya Ringspot Virus (PRSV), causing mottled spots on leaves, leaf distortion, fruit distortion, reduced fruit size, and oily spots on fruit surface in severe infections.

pests.name: Leafhoppers
pests.impact: Transmits Phytoplasma disease, causing small, thick, yellowish-green leaves, shortened internodes, stunted plants, phyllody, and greening of flowers.

pests.name: Nematodes
pests.impact: Spreads Bacterial Wilt, leading to sudden wilting of leaves, browning and drying of leaves, vascular discoloration, and eventual death of the plant.
pests.solution: No specific solution mentioned for nematodes, but general disease control practices for Bacterial Wilt include destroying infected plants, crop rotation, intercropping, and avoiding movement of contaminated equipment.

pests.name: Insects
pests.impact: Spreads Bacterial Wilt, leading to sudden wilting of leaves, browning and drying of leaves, vascular discoloration, and eventual death of the plant.
pests.solution: No specific solution mentioned for insects, but general disease control practices for Bacterial Wilt include destroying infected plants, crop rotation, intercropping, and avoiding movement of contaminated equipment.

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.impact: Initially light yellow spots on mature leaves, which enlarge with white mycelium growth on the leaf surface. Severely affected leaves turn brown, shrivel, and drop.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development. Remove infected crop residues after harvest.

diseases.name: Downy Mildew
diseases.impact: Angular yellow spots on the upper leaf surface, limited by leaf veins. Leaves become chlorotic, eventually necrotic, and host plant cells die.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development. Remove infected plant parts after harvest.

diseases.name: Root Rot / Foot Rot
diseases.impact: In early stages, plants show temporary wilting symptoms. Leaves turn yellow, wilt, and droop. Eventually, the plant dies. Vascular bundles at the base turn yellow or brown. White fungal mycelium can be seen at the base of the plant.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants along with the soil. Improve drainage. Use recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development.

diseases.name: Gummy Stem Blight
diseases.impact: Lesions typically start in water-soaked areas on leaves and fruits. Early stages may show chlorotic holes, which then turn light brown and become irregular in shape. Dark, cracked, sunken spots appear on fruits. Sunken cankers with gum exudation occur on stems, often appearing as streaks. Dark brown to black pycnidia can be seen on fruits, stems, or leaves.
diseases.solution: Deep plow crop residues immediately after harvest to reduce fungal survival. Use recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development.

diseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
diseases.impact: Sudden wilting of leaves is observed. Eventually, the entire plant wilts. Leaves turn brown and dry. Vascular discoloration (browning) occurs. Leads to permanent wilting.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants along with the soil. Practice crop rotation with resistant varieties (okra, maize). Intercrop with Cruciferae. Avoid moving equipment from infected fields to uninfected fields.

diseases.name: Leaf Curl Virus (LCV)
diseases.impact: Upward curling of leaves, shortening of internodes, and distortion. Stunted plants and deformed fruits.

diseases.name: Papaya Ringspot Virus (PRSV)
diseases.impact: Mottled spots on leaves. Leaf distortion. Fruit distortion and reduced fruit size. In severe infections, oily spots appear on the fruit surface.

diseases.name: Cucumber Mosaic Virus (CMV)
diseases.impact: Green mottling and leaf distortion. Green spots on fruits, which turn bright yellow and decay. Fruit distortion.
diseases.solution: No specific solution mentioned in the document.

diseases.name: Zucchini Yellow Mosaic Virus (ZYMV)
diseases.impact: Stunted plants, yellowing, chlorosis (occasionally), mottled and distorted leaves (blistering). Fruit discoloration and distortion.
diseases.solution: No specific solution mentioned in the document.

diseases.name: Phytoplasma disease / Little Leaf disease
diseases.impact: Small, thick, and yellowish-green leaves. Shortening of internodes and stunted plants. Phyllody (chrysanthemum-like appearance). Greening of flowers.
diseases.solution: Remove infected plants. Control vectors (leafhoppers) using recommended insecticides.
